metadata.note: All openEHR internal terminology code systems. terminology_id = 'openehr' in compositions.

concepts.definition: A new version was created for the first time.
Clinical content was corrected or updated after original creation.
Non-clinical or structural change (e.g. metadata update) without changing clinical meaning.
A new version was derived by combining or aggregating data from multiple sources.
The version was marked as deleted (logical delete; data is retained in the audit trail).
A clinician signed or witnessed the record, creating an attestation version.
A previously deleted version was restored to active status.
The record was converted from one technical format or schema to another without clinical change.
The type of change is not known.
Composition intended to be kept indefinitely, independent of any specific episode of care (e.g. problem list, medication list, allergies).
Composition scoped to a specific episode of care or encounter (e.g. discharge summary, operative report).
Composition recording a point-in-time clinical event or observation within an encounter (e.g. vital signs, lab result).
Composition that is a formal clinical report, typically produced at the end of an episode or investigation.

code_systems.name: event_math_function
code_systems.description: Event Math Function — used in INTERVAL_EVENT.math_function

concepts.definition: The lowest value observed over the interval.
The highest value observed over the interval.
The most frequently occurring value over the interval.
The middle value of the distribution over the interval.
The arithmetic average of values over the interval.
The net change (end minus start) over the interval.
The sum of all values accumulated over the interval.
The statistical variation (variance or range) of values over the interval.
Indicates the value decreased over the interval.
Indicates the value increased over the interval.
A single actual measurement taken at a specific point within the interval (not aggregated).

code_systems.name: instruction_states
code_systems.description: Instruction States — used in ISM_TRANSITION.current_state. This state machine is the result of long-term experience with clinical workflows and act management systems.

concepts.definition: Initial state, prior to planning activity. Default starting state for computable representation of the state machine.
The action has been described but has not yet been performed.
The action has not been performed and will not be without specific conditions being met. Events that would normally activate the instruction are ignored until a restore event occurs.
The action was defined but was cancelled before being performed.
The action will be performed at some designated future time and has been booked in a scheduling system.
The action is being performed according to its definition. The entire course of a medication or therapy corresponds to this state.
The action was begun but has been stopped temporarily and will not be restarted until explicitly resumed.
The action began but was permanently terminated before normal completion.
The action began and was completed normally.
The time during which the action could have been relevant has expired; the action may have completed, been cancelled, or never occurred.
Trigger that moves the instruction from initial to planned state.
An intermediate planning step while the instruction remains in planned state.
Defer the instruction; moves from planned to postponed state.
Reinstate a postponed instruction back to planned state.
Abandon the instruction before it begins; moves to cancelled state.
An activity recorded while the instruction is in postponed state.
Book the action in a scheduling system; moves from planned to scheduled state.
An activity recorded while the instruction is in scheduled state.
Begin execution of the instruction; moves from scheduled (or planned) to active state.
Perform one step of an active instruction (e.g. administer one dose).
Record an interim activity while the instruction remains active.
Temporarily pause an active instruction; moves to suspended state.
An activity recorded while the instruction is in suspended state.
Restart a suspended instruction; returns to active state.
Permanently stop an active or suspended instruction; moves to aborted state.
Successfully complete an active instruction; moves to completed state.
The instruction expired because its time window passed; moves to expired state.
Notification event that the instruction was aborted (used for workflow messaging).
Notification event that the instruction was completed (used for workflow messaging).
Notification event that the instruction was cancelled (used for workflow messaging).
Critically high — value is dangerously above the normal range, requires immediate attention.
Significantly high — value is notably above the normal range.
High — value is above the upper boundary of the normal range.
Normal — value is within the expected reference range.
Low — value is below the lower boundary of the normal range.
Significantly low — value is notably below the normal range.
Critically low — value is dangerously below the normal range, requires immediate attention.

code_systems.name: null_flavours
code_systems.description: Null Flavours — used when a value is absent

concepts.definition: No information is available about the value — it was never captured or recorded.
The value exists but is not known at the time of documentation.
The value exists and is known but is withheld for privacy or security reasons.
The concept does not apply to this patient or context (e.g. last menstrual period for a male patient).

code_systems.name: participation_mode
code_systems.description: Participation Mode — how the party participated

concepts.definition: The mode of participation was not recorded.
The party was physically present and communicated directly in person.
Face-to-face communication with a language interpreter present.
Face-to-face communication conducted in sign language.
Real-time audio and video communication (e.g. telehealth video call).
Multi-party real-time video call.
Two-party real-time video call.
Communication by telephone (voice only).
Multi-party telephone conference call.
Asynchronous communication via email.
The party was present at the care location but their communication mode was not separately specified.
The party participated from a different physical location (mode of remote contact not specified).
Care delivered in the patient's own home, including home visits and telehealth from home.
Acute unplanned care in an emergency department or urgent care centre.
Outpatient GP/family physician consultation in a primary care setting.
Nursing-led care in a community or primary care context.
Allied health (physiotherapy, occupational therapy, etc.) in a primary care context.
Midwife-led antenatal, intrapartum, or postnatal care.
Inpatient or specialist outpatient care in a hospital or secondary care facility.
Nursing care delivered in a hospital inpatient ward.
Allied health delivered in a hospital or secondary care setting.
Complementary or alternative medicine consultation (e.g. acupuncture, homeopathy).
Care delivered by a dentist or dental specialist.
Care in a residential aged care or nursing home facility.
Psychiatric or psychological care, inpatient or outpatient.
Any other care setting not covered by the above categories.
The subject of the data is the patient themselves (most common case).
The subject is an unborn foetus; data recorded on behalf of the mother.
The related party is the patient's mother (used for family history entries).
The related party is the patient's father (used for family history entries).
The related party is an organ or tissue donor.
The relationship is not known.
The related party is the patient's partner or spouse.
The related party is the patient's brother.
The related party is the patient's sister.
The related party is a sibling of the patient (sex unspecified).
The related party is a child of the patient (sex unspecified).
The related party is the patient's daughter.
The related party is the patient's son.
The related party is a parent of the patient (sex unspecified).
The related party is the patient's biological mother (distinct from adoptive/legal mother).
The related party is the patient's biological father (distinct from adoptive/legal father).
The subject is a newborn; data recorded in the neonatal period shortly after birth.
